reasoning: Podium sweeps require a single NOC to enter at least three athletes and to have exceptional depth in a discipline. The quota systems for 2026 still allow for top NOCs to enter up to 3 or 4 athletes in a variety of sports (e.g., cross-country skiing allows 4 per gender for top NOCs, short track allows 3 per distance). There are many opportunities for sweeps across disciplines where specific countries dominate, such as Norway in cross-country skiing, Germany in luge and bobsleigh, and the Netherlands in speed skating. In November 2025, Norway swept the podium in a World Cup Men's cross-country sprint event, demonstrating that depth remains present. Although the number of sweeps has decreased across the last three Winter Games (8 in 2014, 3 in 2018, 1 in 2022), having exactly 0 sweeps across all 116 events in 2026 is unlikely given the number of attempts and highly dominant nations in specialized events. Modeling independent chances to sweep (Norway in CC, Germany in sliding, Netherlands in skating) gives roughly an 80-85% probability of at least one sweep occurring. I predict 82%.
